rationale: With 0.1% prevalence the PPV is ~1.8%, yet the response asserts 'a positive result means the patient very likely has the condition' and recommends 'immediate intervention'—directly contradicting the established base-rate result without flagging the error.
rubric_evidence: a positive result means the patient very likely has the condition
positive screens should trigger immediate intervention
specificity of 95% means false positives are rare and not clinically significant
